title: Text and headings
assignment: Review the concepts from this section. If something is unclear, go back to the lesson.
groups.0.title: The hierarchy
groups.0.items.0.def: <h1> says what the WHOLE page is about. There is one. If everything is h1, the page shouts equally loud about everything — and both Google and a blind person get lost.
groups.0.items.1.def: <h1> to <h6>, like a book's contents: chapter, section, subsection. You do not skip levels. And you do not pick the level by how big you want it — you pick it by importance. The size is a consequence.
groups.0.items.2.def: . Each sentence — its own paragraph, not one shared. The blank lines in your code do not exist for the browser; if you want separation, you say so with a tag.
groups.1.title: Block and inline
groups.1.items.0.def:
Takes a whole line and pushes the next thing down:

,

. Two blocks will NEVER stand side by side, no matter how you arrange them.
groups.1.items.1.def: Lives inside the line, between words, does not break it: <strong>, <em>, <a>. A block is a line, an inline is a word.
groups.1.items.2.def: <strong> does not mean "bold" — it means "important". <em> does not mean "slanted" — it means "stress". If you want only the look, that is CSS's job. The tag is chosen by meaning.
groups.2.title: More from this section
groups.2.items.0.def: <del> = no longer valid (comes out struck through). <ins> = added. <mark> = highlighted. <small> = the fine print. Again by meaning, not look — change the style tomorrow and the meaning must not go with it.
groups.2.items.1.def:
is a new line inside a paragraph — an address, a verse. Not for space between paragraphs.
is a change of topic. Both are void — no closing tag.
groups.2.items.2.def: How you write a character that is otherwise code. < for <, > for >, & for &. Otherwise the browser takes < as the start of a tag.   is a space that does not break.
